title: Spanish Literature
shortSummary: Read and appreciate Spanish literature — from Don Quijote to García Márquez, Borges to Isabel Allende. Develop reading comprehension and cultural literacy through the greatest works in Spanish.
whyItMatters: Literature is the highest expression of a language. Reading in Spanish develops vocabulary, grammatical intuition, and cultural understanding that no textbook can provide. It also opens up one of the world's richest literary traditions.
coreConcepts: Reading strategies for literature in Spanish
Key authors: Cervantes, García Márquez, Borges, Allende, Neruda
Literary movements: Siglo de Oro, Boom Latinoamericano, Realismo Mágico
Poetry in Spanish: rhythm, rhyme, and metre
The relationship between literature and cultural identity
